ucs.title: [LLM] UNC3886 malicious VIB install / acceptance-level downgrade on ESXi (VIRTUALPITA/VIRTUALPIE staging)
ucs.description: Hunts for the exact esxcli command patterns UNC3886 uses to drop VIRTUALPITA and VIRTUALPIE backdoors as malicious vSphere Installation Bundles, including forced installs of unsigned VIBs and downgrading the host acceptance level to CommunitySupported. This is the signature persistence move tied to G1048 by Mandiant.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.dest) as host from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=esxcli OR Processes.process=*esxcli*) AND (Processes.process="*software vib install*" OR Processes.process="*software acceptance set*") AND (Processes.process="*--force*" OR Processes.process="*--no-sig-check*" OR Processes.process="*CommunitySupported*" OR Processes.process="*PartnerSupported*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user,"(?i)^(svc_vmwarepatch|vmware-update)$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousVibCmd = dynamic(["software vib install","software acceptance set","software vib remove"]);
let riskFlags = dynamic(["--force","--no-sig-check","CommunitySupported","PartnerSupported"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ ("esxcli","esxcli.exe","plink.exe","ssh","ssh.exe","putty.exe")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any (suspiciousVibCmd)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (suspiciousVibCmd)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (riskFlags)
| extend HostType = iff(DeviceName has_any("vcenter","vcsa","esx"),"vSphereMgmt","AdminJump")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, HostType, AccountName, AccountDomain, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl=tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).RemoteUrl)
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemotePort == 22 or RemotePort == 443 | project DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ConnTime=Timestamp) on DeviceName
| where ConnTime between (Timestamp - 10m .. Timestamp + 10m) or isempty(ConnTime)
| summarize Hits=count(), CmdLines=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,10), TargetESXi=make_set(RemoteIP,10) by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp,1h)
ucs.rationale: UNC3886 (Mandiant G1048, China-nexus) is uniquely characterized by hypervisor-level persistence: they were the first publicly tracked actor to weaponize malicious VIBs (VIRTUALPITA, VIRTUALPIE) and routinely flip host acceptance level so unsigned bundles install. Generic APTs don't touch esxcli — the combination of `vib install --force/--no-sig-check` plus an acceptance downgrade is essentially a

ucs.title: [LLM] UNC3886 vCenter→ESXi pivot with TINYSHELL/REPTILE callback from hypervisor management VMK
ucs.description: Detects the post-exploitation chain UNC3886 uses after compromising vCenter (CVE-2023-34048) or Fortinet edge devices: extract vpxuser credentials, SSH from vCenter to managed ESXi hosts, then have the hypervisor itself beacon outbound TINYSHELL/REPTILE C2 over non-standard ports — an outbound flow that should never originate from an ESXi vmkernel interface.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as ports values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.src_category="esxi_vmk" OR All_Traffic.src_category="vcenter" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | search NOT [| inputlookup esxi_allowed_egress.csv | fields dest] | eval beacon_score=if((dest_port>1024 AND dest_port!=443 AND dest_port!=53),3,1) | where beacon_score>=3 OR dest_port IN (1080,1443,8443,53413) | join type=left src [| tstats summariesonly=true count as ssh_in from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=22 by All_Traffic.dest | rename All_Traffic.dest as src] | where ssh_in>0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let esxiSubnets = dynamic(["10.20.0.0/16","10.30.0.0/16"]); // tune to your mgmt VMK ranges
let vcenterHosts = dynamic(["vcsa01","vcsa02"]);
let sshFromVcenter =
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RemotePort == 22
| where DeviceName in~ (vcenterHosts) or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("ssh","plink.exe","sshd")
| where ipv4_is_in_any_range(RemoteIP, esxiSubnets)
| project SshTime=Timestamp, vCenter=DeviceName, ESXi_IP=RemoteIP, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let esxiEgress =
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where ipv4_is_in_any_range(LocalIP, esxiSubnets)
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where RemotePort !in (53,80,123,443,514,902)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
| project EgressTime=Timestamp, ESXi_IP=LocalIP, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl, BytesSent=tolong(parse_json(AdditionalFields).bytes_sent);
sshFromVcenter
| join kind=inner esxiEgress on ESXi_IP
| where EgressTime between (SshTime .. SshTime + 4h)
| summarize FirstSSH=min(SshTime), FirstEgress=min(EgressTime), C2Ports=make_set(RemotePort,10), C2IPs=make_set(RemoteIP,10), TotalBytes=sum(BytesSent) by vCenter, ESXi_IP, AccountName
| where TotalBytes > 0 or array_length(C2Ports) >= 1
ucs.rationale: ESXi hypervisors should never initiate outbound traffic to the internet — they pull from vCenter and patch repos only. UNC3886's tradecraft (per Mandiant and Google TIC) is to land on vCenter via CVE-2023-34048 / VMCI, hop to ESXi over SSH using stolen vpxuser hashes, then run TINYSHELL/REPTILE rootkits whose C2 originates from the vmkernel interface. The correlation of `vCenter→ESXi SSH` followed
